case_name: Herbert Moore v. Sonu G. Singh

summary.brief: Herbert Moore v. Sonu G. Singh. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: Rosemary Moore, who as a result of her diabetes had end-stage renal disease, was transported by ambulance to Henry Medical Center Emergency Department
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rosemary was diagnosed with a left ankle sprain and left knee contusion and discharged from the hospital that same day
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Stephan Borkan provided expert testimony that a nephrologist examining Rosemary would have been concentrating on the status of her bones
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Stephan Borkan

evidence_index.text: Dr. Orcutt testified that when he first saw Rosemary in late March 2009, the fracture was at the least six weeks old
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Daniel Orcutt

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the trial court erred by granting the motion for directed verdict.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Moore presented evidence creating a jury issue as to whether Dr. Singh would have discovered the fracture if she had properly complied with the standard of care
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Dr. Orcutt testified that if the fracture had been detected while it was non-displaced, then it could have been treated without surgical intervention
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Daniel Orcutt

evidence_index.text: Rosemary continued to experience issues with her leg and was unable to bear weight on it.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Judgment reversed. McFadden and Boggs, JJ., concur.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Unable to put weight on left leg after a fall
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Rosemary Moore, with end-stage renal disease due to diabetes, fell at home on December 23, 2008, hitting her left knee on a door frame. She was transported to the emergency department.

focused_exam.lower_extremity: Tenderness and swelling in the left leg, particularly around the knee

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Left ankle sprain and left knee contusion

requestables.name: X-ray of left knee
requestables.description: Radiographic imaging of the left knee to assess for fractures

reveal.clinical_significance: Initial assessment did not reveal fracture, but fracture could be occult
reveal.decision_impact: Further imaging or follow-up required if symptoms persist

requestables.name: CT scan or MRI of left leg
requestables.description: Advanced imaging to detect occult fractures

requestables.name: Orthopedic consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with an orthopedic specialist to evaluate leg pain

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have led to diagnosis of fracture

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED after fall with leg pain

timeline_phases.name: Hospitalization and Follow-up
timeline_phases.description: Patient hospitalized for further assessment and management

key_events.event: Patient complains of persistent leg pain

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with persistent left leg pain and inability to bear weight
decision_points.prompt: What further investigations should be ordered to assess the cause of leg pain?

options.description: Order CT scan or MRI of the left leg
options.clinical_reasoning: Advanced imaging is necessary to detect occult fractures not visible on X-ray

options.description: Continue with conservative management without further imaging

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not order further imaging

expected_action_court.description: CT scan or MRI should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated advanced imaging was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order further imaging led to missed diagnosis of fracture
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Advanced imaging would have detected the fracture
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient required surgery for displaced fracture
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Complicated recovery due to delayed diagnosis

key_findings: Directed verdict initially granted for defendant
Appeal found jury issue on causation, reversed directed verdict

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order CT or MRI
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Advanced imaging would have detected fracture

factual_timeline.event: Patient falls and injures left knee

complications.complication: Delayed diagnosis leading to complicated surgery

allegations.allegation: Failure to order CT or MRI
